hero.question: US forces enter Iran by March 31?
hero.prob_take: An 8% probability is the market saying "this won't happen" with high confidence, pricing in only black swan scenarios like a regional escalation spiral or a dramatic shift in administration policy. The real question is whether 8% is too low given the number of moving pieces in the Middle East right now, or whether it's actually fair value for an outcome that requires simultaneous political will, military readiness, and no major diplomatic off-ramps between now and March 31.

trade.question: Will the U.S. invade Iran by March 31?

daily_take.headline: 8% Iran Strike Odds Look Absurdly Cheap on Escalation Trajectory
daily_take.deck: US-Iran futures dropped half a point overnight, but the move masks a market underpricing systematic escalation risk. With $32.5M in volume and current tensions, smart money sees this as a 3-to-1 value opportunity. The question isn't if conflict happens, it's whether markets are extrapolating the right baseline.

sidebar.headline: Yemen strike probability jumped 4 points in 24 hours. This isn't noise. Connected markets moving together signal real geopolitical heat that Iran odds haven't fully priced.
sidebar.label: Israel-Yemen Strike: 25.5%

sidebar.headline: Arizona's tournament odds hit 36.2% after a 2-point rip. March Madness still 13 months out, but early sharp action on blue-blood programs compounds through the year.
sidebar.label: Arizona 2026 NCAA: 36.2%

sidebar.headline: Trump removal odds barely budged at 13.3%. Political risk markets are sleeping while legal and health uncertainty stack. This needs watching harder.
sidebar.label: Trump Out Before 2027: 13.3%
